intro: The clearest split between Accessibility Insights and Level Access is cost: Accessibility Insights is free to use, while Level Access only offers custom / contact-sales pricing. Accessibility Insights is Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks for web and Windows. Level Access is enterprise accessibility compliance program with audits, training, and managed services. Pick Accessibility Insights when the job is developers and qa doing per-page or per-feature accessibility reviews and you accept high lock-in; pick Level Access when it is regulated enterprises needing full-stack a11y platform plus services and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Accessibility Insights at fully public pricing and documentation with solid developer experience, and Level Access at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Accessibility Insights is a poor fit for teams needing continuous monitoring across hundreds of pages, and Level Access is a poor fit for startups and smbs wanting a lightweight scanner. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Accessibility Insights is for Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks; Level Access is for enterprise accessibility compliance program; decide based on budget vs. support.
verdict_a: Choose Accessibility Insights if cost is the binding constraint and the work maps to Microsoft's free automated and guided accessibility checks for web and Windows.
verdict_b: Choose Level Access if some ecosystem coupling is acceptable and the work lines up with enterprise accessibility compliance program with audits, training, and managed services.

faq.q: Is Accessibility Insights cheaper than Level Access?
faq.a: Neither Accessibility Insights nor Level Access publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Accessibility Insights uses a free model and Level Access uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Accessibility Insights to Level Access?
faq.a: Leaving Accessibility Insights for Level Access is the harder direction: Accessibility Insights is rated high lock-in, so exporting data and rebuilding integrations is the main cost. Going the other way (Level Access to Accessibility Insights) is usually easier.

Accessibility Insights scores higher on developer experience in our data (4/5 vs 3/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Level Access a good alternative to Accessibility Insights?
faq.a: Yes — Level Access is a reasonable alternative to Accessibility Insights for developers and qa doing per-page or per-feature accessibility reviews.. The practical differences are free-vs-custom billing and high-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Level Access as a credible swap.
